projects.title: sim-arm
projects.tagline: A physics-based simulator for 6-DOF robotic manipulators — rigid-body dynamics, contact, and real-time control in a single loop.

sections.label: // overview
paragraphs: sim-arm is a from-scratch simulator for articulated robot arms. It integrates full rigid-body dynamics, resolves contacts and joint limits, and runs a configurable control loop fast enough to drive hardware in the loop.
I built it to test motion-planning and control ideas without risking real hardware — and to keep the physics legible: every force, constraint, and integration step is inspectable and logged.

sections.label: // how it works
paragraphs: A semi-implicit Euler integrator advances joint positions and velocities each step. Constraints are projected using a sequential-impulse solver tuned for small articulated chains.

projects.title: kalman-rs
projects.tagline: A small, well-typed Kalman/EKF/UKF library in Rust — focused on legibility and zero-allocation hot paths.

sections.label: // overview
paragraphs: kalman-rs is a generic implementation of common Bayesian filters with a focus on numerical stability and ergonomics.

projects.title: nbody-gpu
projects.tagline: An n-body gravitational simulator that pushes millions of particles per second through a tiled CUDA kernel.

sections.label: // overview
paragraphs: A tiled, shared-memory CUDA implementation of the all-pairs n-body problem with Barnes-Hut as a planned follow-up.
